maintainer_role: Primary maintainer responsible for product direction, implementation, documentation, issue triage, release planning, and security response.
project_mission: Help open-source maintainers prepare honest, evidence-backed support applications and maintenance health reports without exaggerating adoption or hiding repository gaps.
ecosystem_impact: The project targets maintainers of small and growing open-source repositories who need a repeatable way to collect governance, security, CI, test, release, and activity evidence before applying to support programs.
credit_usage_plan: Use AI coding and API credits to review pull requests, summarize issue queues, improve test coverage, draft release notes, inspect security-sensitive changes, and generate public maintenance reports for open-source projects.
additional_context: The project is intentionally evidence-first: generated drafts include placeholders when usage or maintainer claims are not yet verified.
public_evidence: Includes a working Python CLI with Markdown, JSON, and HTML reports.
Includes a maintainer evidence pack generator with application draft and checklist outputs.
Includes governance files, security policy, contribution guide, issue templates, CI, tests, and an initial release tag.
